title: Network Evidence, Safety, and Troubleshooting Method · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Operate a safe virtual help-desk lab with tickets, diagrams, baselines, commands, packet evidence, and change records. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a reproducible network evidence notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement or escalate, verify, prevent, and document steps to one network incident.
Separate user report, observed symptom, affected scope, recent change, and reproducible condition before forming a theory.
Form one falsifiable probable-cause theory and isolate it with bottom-up, top-down, or divide-and-conquer evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

stimulus.title: Net Incident Documentation case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network evidence method. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Command history alone is a complete and understandable incident record.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record timeline, environment, commands, findings, actions, outcomes, lessons, and evidence without exposing secrets. Artifact under review: a complete first version of a reproducible network evidence notebook while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Net Incident Documentation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Incident Documentation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record timeline, environment, commands, findings, actions, outcomes, lessons, and evidence without exposing secrets.
